steps.title: Use exponential growth form
steps.content:
For yearly exponential growth, use the starting amount as the coefficient and the annual growth factor as the base.
$$p(t)=a(1+r)^t$$

steps.title: Substitute the starting values
steps.content:
The initial population is $30$. A $14.8\%$ yearly increase means $r=0.148$, so the growth factor is $1.148$.
$$p(t)=30(1.148)^t$$

steps.title: Match the model
steps.content: The model with coefficient $30$ and base $1.148$ is the correct exponential function. The answer is C.
